data.name: Equinix Corpnet - NAMER
data.notes: The Equinix Corporate network (AS14609) is being integrated into [as15830] (https://www.peeringdb.com/asn/15830) and existing IX connections replaced by new connections to [as15830] (https://www.peeringdb.com/asn/15830).

data.name: Equinix Internet Exchange - MLPE Route Servers
data.name_long: Multi-Lateral Peering Exchange, IX  route server peering
data.notes:
The Equinix MLPE service automates peering for open networks by providing route servers for participants to exchange routing information with multiple peers using only a pair of BGP sessions. 

This allows for the establishment of sessions without a bilateral peering agreement in place with the participating networks, The MLPE route server peering is available at all Equinix Internet Exchange locations.  

All Equinix MLPE route servers support the ability for participants to more granularly control outbound announcements of their routes using BGP Communities:

[MLPE BGP Communities info] (https://docs.equinix.com/en-us/Content/Interconnection/IX/IX-mlpe-bgp-communities.htm)

Please note that despite the unified ASN, the Equinix MLPE service does not redistribute prefixes between Internet Exchanges in different metros.

IX participants can subscribe to the MLPE  route server peering using the IX Portal. 

[ix.equinix.com/ixp/mlpeRegistration] (https://ix.equinix.com/ixp/mlpeRegistration)

data.name: NYIIX-NY Route Servers
data.notes:
**NYIIX-NY Route Servers:**

- 198.32.160.1/23 (primary)
- 198.32.160.2/23 (secondary)
- 2001:504:1::a501:3538:1/64 (primary)
- 2001:504:1::a501:3538:2 /64(secondary)
- ASN 13538

**Communities Supported:**

**Do Not Advertise to peer-as**
- 0:peer-as (standard)
- rt:0:peer-as (extended)	
- ro:0:peer-as (extended)
- 13538:0:peer-as (large)

**Advertise to peer-as**
- 13538:peer-as (standard)
- rt:13538:peer-as (extended)	
- ro:13538:peer-as (extended)
- 13538:1:peer-as (large)

**Do Not Advertise to any peer**
- 0:13538 (standard)
- rt:0:13538 (extended)	
- ro:0:13538 (extended)
- 13538:0:0 (large)

**Advertise to any peer**
- 13538:13538 (standard)
- rt:13538:13538 (extended)	
- ro:13538:13538 (extended)
- 13538:1:13538 (large)

data.name: Any2 Route Servers - Any2West
data.notes:
CoreSite Any2Easy automates peering for open networks by providing route servers for participants to exchange routes with multiple peers using only a pair of BGP sessions. This ASN is for Any2West.

This allows for traffic to be exchanged without the need for a bilateral peering agreement in place with the participating networks.  This service is provided in most markets and is complementary as part of your Any2 service.

Registration is fast and easy!
https://www.coresite.com/interconnection/peering-exchanges/any2-internet-peering-exchange/any2easy-sign-up

data.name: Any2 Route Servers
data.notes:
CoreSite Any2Easy automates peering for open networks by providing route servers for participants to exchange routes with multiple peers using only a pair of BGP sessions. This ASN is for Any2East, Any2Chicago, Any2Denver, and Any2Flordia

This allows for traffic to be exchanged without the need for a bilateral peering agreement in place with the participating networks.  This service is provided in most markets and is complementary as part of your Any2 service.

Registration is fast and easy!
https://www.coresite.com/interconnection/peering-exchanges/any2-internet-peering-exchange/any2easy-sign-up

data.name: Renesys

data.name: Rackspace
data.notes:
Rackspace US and Europe has consolidated to our new backbone AS12200. 
Please note in Europe we have traffic requirements of 5 Mbps (95th percentile).

data.name: TorIX Services Network
data.notes: This is the TorIX Services network which includes a globally reachable stratum 1 NTP cluster, AS112 node, a NL-NOG RING node and of course the TorIX e-mail and web servers.  We recommend TorIX peers who do not use the route-servers request direct peering; all others can reach us via our announcements to the route-servers.

data.name: Stealth Communications
data.notes:
PNI Preferred.  Interface Options: 
100G LR4; 400G LR4; 800G FR4.

data.name: Yahoo
data.notes:
We require sessions to be built to both IPs for all dual-attached public exchange points.  

Peers that only establish adjacencies with only one router may be eliminated for non-compliance.

We only support 10G and 100G private peering connections.

We prefer direct BGP sessions whenever possible. Routes learned from route-server sessions only may not provide optimal traffic exchange.

data.name: AOL
data.aka: AOL Transit Data Network (ATDN), Oath, Verizon Media
data.notes:
1668/10310 managed by Oath Global Network Team - 

1668 is no longer accepting new peering requests. You can reach 1668 by peering 10310.

Please contact peering-requests@oath.com for questions.
